Defines the EvalError.prototype built-in object and its properties for JerryScript.

# Purpose
This code is a C header file that defines the properties and identifiers for the `EvalError.prototype` object in a JavaScript engine. It uses macros to declare object identifiers and properties associated with the `EvalError` prototype, which is part of the ECMAScript standard. The `OBJECT_ID` macro assigns an identifier to the `EvalError.prototype`, while the `OBJECT_VALUE` and `STRING_VALUE` macros define the properties of the prototype, such as `constructor`, `name`, and `message`. These properties are marked as configurable and writable, allowing them to be modified in JavaScript. The file concludes by undefining the macros to prevent conflicts with other code.
